subsection: KIT mutation in core-binding factor AML
title: KIT mutation in RUNX1::RUNX1T1 or CBFB::MYH11 AML
learning_objectives: Distinguish diagnostic, prognostic and clonal markers
clinical_vignette: A 39-year-old man has AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1. NGS detects a KIT exon 17 mutation. FLT3-ITD and NPM1 are negative.
question: What is the best interpretation of the KIT mutation?
options: A. It changes the diagnosis to AML with KIT mutation.
B. It is a prognostic modifier in core-binding factor AML but does not replace the fusion-defined diagnosis.
C. It proves acute lymphoblastic leukaemia.
E. It defines acute promyelocytic leukaemia.
correct_answer: B. It is a prognostic modifier in core-binding factor AML but does not replace the fusion-defined diagnosis.
explanation: KIT mutations may influence relapse risk in core-binding factor AML but the diagnostic entity remains AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1 or CBFB::MYH11.
frcpath_pearl: Separate diagnostic entity from relapse-risk modifier.
take_home_message: Molecular findings in AML must be interpreted in diagnostic hierarchy and clinical context.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition
